evals.prompt: My TypeScript build is failing with 'Cannot find module' errors after I moved some files around. Debug this.
evals.expected_output: Parallel debug investigation spawning all three investigation agents with structured debug report output
expectations: log-investigator agent is spawned to check for build error logs
state-investigator agent is spawned to check config and imports
git-investigator agent is spawned to analyze recent file moves
All three agents are spawned in parallel (not sequentially)
Output follows the structured debug report format (Problem Statement, Error Analysis, Investigation Findings, Root Cause Hypothesis, Suggested Fix Approach, Files to Examine)
No files are modified during investigation (read-only rule)

evals.prompt: Debug this: npm test is failing with 3 test failures after the last commit. The tests were passing before.
evals.expected_output: Debug report with actionable root cause hypothesis and specific fix suggestions
expectations: Error analysis section parses test failure output (failing test names, assertion errors, file locations)
Root cause hypothesis is clearly stated as a hypothesis, not a certainty
Suggested fix approach includes numbered steps (at least 2 alternatives)
Files to Examine section lists specific file:line references
git-investigator checks the last commit for regression-causing changes

evals.prompt: A Spectrum quality gate failed during STORY-005 execution. The typecheck gate returned errors. Debug the failure for the next retry iteration.
evals.expected_output: Debug investigation formatted for Spectrum integration with progress.md-compatible output
expectations: stories.json is read to understand STORY-005 context
progress.md is read for recent history
Output follows the Spectrum debug progress entry format (Timestamp, Quality Gate Failed, Error Output, Investigation Findings, Root Cause, Suggested Fix)
Findings are formatted for appending to progress.md
Typecheck error locations (file:line) are parsed and listed
